---
title: "VMS Fishing Explorer — Los Lagos"
subtitle: "Interactive map of artisanal fleet activity 2019–2026"
date: 2026-04-09
author: "Roberto Cárdenas-Retamal"
categories:
  - R
  - Fisheries
  - Chile
  - Interactive
  - VMS
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    embed-resources: false
resources:
  - data/cell_annual.csv
  - data/ring_annual.csv
  - data/monthly_trend.csv
  - vms-app.html
---

<p class="back-nav"><a href="index.qmd">← Back to Project</a></p>

This tool explores SERNAPESCA vessel monitoring system (VMS) data for the
artisanal fishing fleet in **Los Lagos, Chile** (2019–2026). The dataset
covers ~58.5 million GPS pings. Use the three tabs to explore spatial
patterns, distance gradients from aquaculture farms, and temporal trends.

::: {.callout-note}
The app runs entirely in your browser — no server required. Data is fetched
from this site (~2 MB) so the first load may take a few seconds.
:::

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="vms-app.html"
  width="100%"
  height="710px"
  style="border:1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius:8px; margin-top:6px;"
  title="VMS Fishing Explorer">
</iframe>
```

---

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Data and methods

- **Source:** SERNAPESCA Vessel Monitoring System (VMS), artisanal fleet
- **Period:** 2019–2026 · **Region:** Los Lagos (lat –44.5° to –40.5°)
- **Fishing:** speed 0.1–3.0 knots (Behivoke et al. 2021)
- **Grid:** 0.05° × 0.05° cells. **Farm distances:** to nearest marine concession centroid.
- Note: 2019–2022 from admin snapshots (~4×/day, ~1,000 vessels); 2023+ from daily artisanal reports (~175–190 vessels).
:::
